title: Codex hooks configuration
description:
Configuration for OpenAI Codex lifecycle hooks.
https://developers.openai.com/codex/hooks

hooks.description: Hook event registrations grouped by Codex lifecycle event.

SessionStart.description: Runs when a Codex session starts. The matcher filters the start source, such as startup, resume, or clear.

PreToolUse.description: Runs before supported tool calls. The matcher filters tool names and aliases.

PermissionRequest.description: Runs before Codex asks for approval. The matcher filters tool names and aliases.

PostToolUse.description: Runs after supported tool calls. The matcher filters tool names and aliases.

PreCompact.description: Runs before conversation compaction. The matcher filters the compaction trigger, such as manual or auto.

PostCompact.description: Runs after conversation compaction. The matcher filters the compaction trigger, such as manual or auto.

UserPromptSubmit.description: Runs before a user prompt is submitted. Codex currently ignores matcher for this event.

SubagentStart.description: Runs when a subagent starts. The matcher filters the subagent type.

SubagentStop.description: Runs when a subagent stops. The matcher filters the subagent type.

Stop.description: Runs when a turn stops. Codex currently ignores matcher for this event.

matcherGroups.description: Matcher groups for one Codex hook event.

matcherGroup.description: Matcher group that decides when one or more hook handlers run.

matcher.description: Regex string that filters when hooks fire. Use *, an empty string, or omit matcher to match every supported occurrence.

hooks.description: Handlers to run when this matcher group matches.

commandHandler.description: Command hook handler. Commands run with the session cwd as their working directory.

type.description: Command handlers are the only hook handler type that runs in the current Codex runtime.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds. Codex uses 600 seconds when omitted.

statusMessage.description: Optional status text shown while the hook runs.

async.description: Parsed by Codex, but async command hooks are not supported yet and async handlers are skipped.

skippedHandler.description: Prompt and agent handlers are parsed by Codex but skipped in the current runtime.
